▸ synchrony idealised gen I · central II · wavelet real data prep EDA III · central IV · P–Q V · C–Q
  • Show All Code
  • Hide All Code

Real-data disturbance–response V — C–Q wavelet synchrony (nitrate concentration → discharge)

The Analysis IV wavelet, moved to the noisier concentration–discharge coupling — nitrate is only partly set by flow, a less-causal, higher-noise test of the method (native-cadence grids, shared physical-time axis)

nic (nic@cascadeclimate.org)

2026-07-18

This is Analysis IV’s method — Morlet wavelet coherence via WaveletComp — moved to the concentration–discharge (C–Q) relationship: driver = discharge Q, response = stream nitrate concentration C. Where the P→Q coupling (III/IV) is close to causal — rain drives flow — the C–Q coupling is partial and noisier: nitrate concentration is only partly set by flow (dilution vs flushing, catchment sources, hysteresis, and — at two of these sites — a chemical manipulation acting directly on nitrate). So this is the real test the study has been building toward: how does the wavelet read a genuinely weaker, less-deterministic coupling?

Both channels are analyzed in log space — the standard C–Q power-law framing (log C = a + b·log Q), so climate = log(Q), log_response = log(C). The pre-fit predictor’s slope below is that C–Q exponent b (≈0 ⇒ chemostatic / well-buffered; ≠0 ⇒ chemodynamic).

Sampling & the shared axis. Nitrate is grab-sampled (weekly to ~3-weekly), so — rather than fabricate a common fine grid — each site runs on its own native-cadence grid (bin = step_days, built in prep_ms_series.Rmd); the wavelet runs in those native steps and every period/lag is rescaled to physical days (× step_days) so all four panels share one period-in-days axis and one annual band. The cost is honest: a coarser-sampled site cannot resolve short periods — hjandrews (21-day steps) goes blank below ~3 months — and the percent of interpolated (no-sample) bins is reported on every panel. MC06 is excluded (its nitrate is 44% empty months with a ~4-year gap — a wavelet there would read interpolation).

Four band-localized quantities, all read at the physical annual band (P≈365 d): wavelet coherence (normalized), cross-power (un-normalized), phase → apparent lag (days), and amplitude ratio C/Q. Same caveats as IV: COI/edge buffering (EF ≈ √2·annual in each site’s steps), and block SEs (one estimate per annual cycle; circular for phase).

Helpers

# ---- small numerics -------------------------------------------------------------------------
CMEAN <- function(a) Arg(mean(exp(1i * a)))            # circular mean of angles
smooth_ma <- function(x, w) { w <- max(3, w); as.numeric(stats::filter(x, rep(1 / w, w))) }

# band scale-rows within ±5% of the annual band — in PHYSICAL days (Period is in native steps, ×step).
band_rows <- function(wc, step) which(abs(wc$Period * step - P_DAYS) <= 0.05 * P_DAYS)

# physical-period guide lines on the coherence image: a season (3 mo), a year, two years.
wc_marks <- c("3 mo" = 91, "1 yr" = 365, "2 yr" = 730)
plot_wc <- function(wc, step, main) {
  op <- par(bg = "white", fg = "black", col.axis = "black", col.lab = "black", col.main = "black")
  on.exit(par(op))
  at_steps <- wc_marks / step                          # physical period → native-step position
  keep <- at_steps >= min(wc$Period) & at_steps <= max(wc$Period)   # only marks the site can resolve
  wc.image(wc, which.image = "wc", plot.arrow = TRUE, plot.contour = FALSE,
           color.key = "interval", timelab = "time (bins)", periodlab = "period",
           legend.params = list(lab = "wavelet coherence"), main = main, graphics.reset = FALSE,
           spec.period.axis = list(at = at_steps[keep], labels = names(wc_marks)[keep]))
  abline(h = log2(at_steps[keep]), lty = 2, col = "black", lwd = 1.4)          # season / year / 2-year guides
}

# ---- WaveletComp wrapper (native steps; dt = 1) ---------------------------------------------
# upperPeriod ≈ 4 years expressed in this site's steps; lowerPeriod = 4 steps (coherence smoothing
# floor ⇒ the site's short-period limit, ≈ 4·step_days). loess.span = 0: analyzed band sits below
# any decadal trend, and standardization is exactly /sd so the ×sd amplitude rescale stays exact.
wco <- function(df, step)
  analyze.coherency(as.data.frame(df[, c("climate", "log_response")]),
                    my.pair = c("climate", "log_response"), loess.span = 0,
                    dt = 1, dj = 1/20, lowerPeriod = 4, upperPeriod = round(4 * P_DAYS / step),
                    make.pval = FALSE, verbose = FALSE)

# ---- collapse to one time-resolved series at the annual band --------------------------------
band_trace <- function(wc, df, step) {
  bi   <- band_rows(wc, step); EF <- round(sqrt(2) * P_DAYS / step)      # COI buffer, in steps
  sd_c <- sd(df$climate); sd_r <- sd(df$log_response)
  ang  <- apply(wc$Angle[bi, , drop = FALSE], 2, CMEAN)
  tibble(time      = df$time, date = df$date, phase = df$phase,
         coherence = colMeans(wc$Coherence[bi, , drop = FALSE]),
         xpower    = colMeans(wc$Power.xy[bi, , drop = FALSE]),
         amp_ratio = colMeans(wc$Ampl.y[bi, , drop = FALSE]) /
                     colMeans(wc$Ampl.x[bi, , drop = FALSE]) * (sd_r / sd_c),
         angle     = ang,
         lag       = ang / (2 * pi) * P_DAYS,                             # phase → DAYS (physical annual band)
         coi_ok    = df$time >= EF & df$time <= nrow(df) - EF)
}

# ---- settled pre/post summary with block SEs (one block per annual cycle, in steps) ---------
block_stat <- function(t, v, blk, circ = FALSE) {
  b  <- (t - min(t)) %/% blk
  bv <- as.numeric(tapply(seq_along(t), b, function(i) if (circ) CMEAN(v[i]) else mean(v[i])))
  n  <- length(bv)
  if (circ) { R <- Mod(mean(exp(1i * bv))); disp <- if (R < 1) sqrt(-2 * log(R)) else 0 } else disp <- sd(bv)
  list(value = if (circ) CMEAN(bv) else mean(bv), se = if (n > 1) disp / sqrt(n) else NA_real_, n = n)
}

# settled windows: buffer EF (steps) from both ends AND the disturbance. blk = annual cycle in steps.
wave_summary <- function(tr, step) {
  EF <- round(sqrt(2) * P_DAYS / step); blk <- max(2, round(P_DAYS / step))
  ds <- min(tr$time[tr$phase == "disturbance"]); de <- max(tr$time[tr$phase == "disturbance"])
  N  <- max(tr$time)
  wins <- list(pre  = tr$time >= EF      & tr$time <= ds - EF,
               post = tr$time >= de + EF & tr$time <= N  - EF)
  one <- function(sel) {
    t <- tr$time[sel]
    co <- block_stat(t, tr$coherence[sel], blk); xp <- block_stat(t, tr$xpower[sel], blk)
    ar <- block_stat(t, tr$amp_ratio[sel], blk); an <- block_stat(t, tr$angle[sel], blk, circ = TRUE)
    tibble(n_blocks = co$n,
           coherence = co$value, coherence_se = co$se,
           xpower    = xp$value, xpower_se    = xp$se,
           amp_ratio = ar$value, amp_ratio_se = ar$se,
           phi       = an$value,                 phi_se = an$se,
           lag       = an$value / (2*pi) * P_DAYS, lag_se = an$se / (2*pi) * P_DAYS)   # days
  }
  bind_rows(pre = one(wins$pre), post = one(wins$post), .id = "phase") %>%
    mutate(phase = factor(phase, c("pre", "post")))
}

# ---- pre-fit C–Q predictor: log(C) ≈ b · log(Q)(t − lag). b = the C–Q power-law exponent -----
fit_pre <- function(df, step) {
  pre <- df[df$phase == "pre", ]; cl <- pre$climate; rs <- pre$log_response; n <- length(rs)
  Ls  <- 0:max(1, round(0.6 * P_DAYS / step))                            # plausible lags < ~0.6 yr, in steps
  cr  <- vapply(Ls, function(L) suppressWarnings(cor(rs[(L + 1):n], cl[seq_len(n - L)])), numeric(1))
  L   <- Ls[which.max(replace(cr, is.na(cr), -Inf))]
  x   <- cl[seq_len(n - L)]; y <- rs[(L + 1):n]
  list(lag = L, gain = sum(x * y) / sum(x * x))
}
resid_trace <- function(df, step) {
  fp <- fit_pre(df, step); L <- fp$lag; N <- nrow(df)
  pred <- rep(NA_real_, N); if (L < N) pred[(L + 1):N] <- fp$gain * df$climate[seq_len(N - L)]
  out <- tibble(time = df$time, date = df$date, phase = df$phase, resid = df$log_response - pred,
                dresid = c(NA, diff(smooth_ma(df$log_response - pred, round(90 / step)))))
  attr(out, "lag") <- L; attr(out, "gain") <- fp$gain; attr(out, "step") <- step; out
}

# ---- shared plotting furniture --------------------------------------------------------------
phase_fill <- c(pre = "#9ecae1", disturbance = "grey45", post = "#fdae6b")
bar_fill   <- phase_fill[c("pre", "post")]
phase_bands <- function(ph) ph %>% filter(phase != "disturbance") %>%
  group_by(phase) %>% summarise(xmin = min(date), xmax = max(date), .groups = "drop")
phase_band_layers <- function(d) {
  bands <- phase_bands(d); td <- d$date[d$phase == "disturbance"]
  list(geom_rect(data = bands, aes(xmin = xmin, xmax = xmax, ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf, fill = phase),
                 alpha = 0.30, inherit.aes = FALSE),
       geom_vline(xintercept = td, linetype = 2, colour = "grey70"),
       scale_fill_manual(values = phase_fill, guide = "none"))
}

plot_resid <- function(df, step, title) {
  rt <- resid_trace(df, step); L <- attr(rt, "lag"); g <- attr(rt, "gain")
  bands <- phase_bands(df); ytop <- max(abs(rt$resid), na.rm = TRUE)
  sc <- ytop / max(abs(rt$dresid), na.rm = TRUE)
  ggplot() + phase_band_layers(df) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, colour = "grey70", linewidth = 0.3) +
    geom_line(data = rt, aes(date, dresid * sc), colour = "#d94801", linewidth = 0.25, na.rm = TRUE) +
    geom_line(data = rt, aes(date, resid),       colour = "#238b45", linewidth = 0.3, na.rm = TRUE) +
    geom_text(data = mutate(bands, x = xmin + (xmax - xmin)/2),
              aes(x = x, y = ytop, label = phase), vjust = 1, size = 3, colour = "grey25") +
    scale_y_continuous(sec.axis = sec_axis(~ . / sc, name = "d(residual)/dt (smoothed, orange)")) +
    labs(title = title, x = NULL, y = "residual = log(C) − predicted (green)",
         subtitle = sprintf("pre-fit C–Q predictor:  log(C)(t) ≈ %.2f · log(Q)(t − %d bins ≈ %d d)   [slope = C–Q exponent b]",
                            g, L, L * step))
}

metric_labs <- c(coherence = "coherence", xpower = "cross power", amp_ratio = "amp ratio", lag = "app. lag (d)")
plot_band_trace <- function(tr, title) {
  m <- names(metric_labs)
  long <- tr %>% mutate(across(all_of(m), ~ ifelse(coi_ok, ., NA_real_))) %>%
    select(date, all_of(m)) %>% pivot_longer(-date, names_to = "metric") %>%
    mutate(metric = factor(metric, m))
  ggplot() + phase_band_layers(tr) +
    geom_line(data = long, aes(date, value), linewidth = 0.3, na.rm = TRUE) +
    facet_wrap(~ metric, scales = "free_y", ncol = 1, strip.position = "left",
               labeller = as_labeller(metric_labs)) +
    labs(title = title, x = NULL, y = NULL)
}

metrics_long <- function(sm, keys) {
  m <- names(metric_labs)
  val <- sm %>% select(all_of(c(keys, m))) %>% pivot_longer(all_of(m), names_to = "metric", values_to = "value")
  se  <- sm %>% select(all_of(c(keys, paste0(m, "_se")))) %>%
    rename_with(~ sub("_se$", "", .x), all_of(paste0(m, "_se"))) %>%
    pivot_longer(all_of(m), names_to = "metric", values_to = "se")
  left_join(val, se, by = c(keys, "metric")) %>% mutate(metric = factor(metric, m))
}
plot_wave_bars <- function(sm, title) {
  ggplot(metrics_long(sm, "phase"), aes(phase, value, fill = phase)) +
    geom_col(width = 0.7) +
    geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = value - se, ymax = value + se), width = 0.25, colour = "black", na.rm = TRUE) +
    facet_wrap(~ metric, scales = "free_y", nrow = 1, labeller = as_labeller(metric_labs)) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = bar_fill, guide = "none") + labs(title = title, x = NULL, y = NULL)
}
emit_table <- function(sm, caption)
  cat(kable(sm %>% select(phase, n_blocks, coherence, xpower, amp_ratio, phi, lag),
            digits = 2, caption = caption), sep = "\n")

# per-site provenance line (n, cadence, % interpolated) — communicated on every panel.
prov_line <- function(d) sprintf(
  "**n = %d bins** (pre %d / post %d) · native step **%d d** · **%.0f%% of bins interpolated** (no NO3 sample).",
  nrow(d), sum(d$phase == "pre"), sum(d$phase == "post"), d$step_days[1], 100 * mean(d$C_interp))
sites <- c("hbef_w1", "hjandrews_GSWS10", "fernow_WS-3", "fernow_WS-5")   # MC06 dropped (see intro)
D <- lapply(sites, function(s)
  read_csv(sprintf("%s__cq_native.csv", s), show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
    mutate(phase = factor(phase, c("pre", "disturbance", "post")),
           climate = log(Q), log_response = log(C)))          # C–Q log–log
names(D) <- sites
STEP <- setNames(vapply(D, function(d) d$step_days[1], numeric(1)), sites)
qd <- read_csv("../qualifying_disturbances.csv", show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
  mutate(site = paste0(domain, "_", site_code))
label <- setNames(sprintf("%s (%s, %s)", qd$site, qd$disturbance, qd$class), qd$site)
# One coherency transform per site (native steps), reused by every image/trace/table.
WC <- Map(wco, D, STEP)
TR <- Map(band_trace, WC, D, STEP)
SM <- Map(wave_summary, TR, STEP)

Data provenance (nitrate coverage)

Nitrate n, native cadence, and interpolated-bin fraction per site — the honest resolution each C–Q wavelet rests on. Discharge is fully observed; the interpolation is all on the nitrate side.

prov <- bind_rows(lapply(D, function(d) tibble(
  site = d$site[1], step_days = d$step_days[1], n_bins = nrow(d),
  pre = sum(d$phase == "pre"), post = sum(d$phase == "post"),
  C_interp_pct = round(100 * mean(d$C_interp), 1))))
cat(kable(prov, caption = "Per-site C–Q grid: native step, n bins, pre/post split, % interpolated (no-sample) bins."), sep = "\n")
Per-site C–Q grid: native step, n bins, pre/post split, % interpolated (no-sample) bins.
site step_days n_bins pre post C_interp_pct
hbef_w1 7 2035 835 1200 5.7
hjandrews_GSWS10 21 747 174 573 3.3
fernow_WS-3 7 1928 311 1617 20.8
fernow_WS-5 7 1473 835 625 9.0

hbef_w1 — chemical addition (pulse) · Ca

The best-sampled site (near-weekly, ~6% interpolated). The Ca (wollastonite) addition acts on watershed chemistry, so pre/post nitrate coupling is squarely in scope here.

cat(prov_line(D[["hbef_w1"]]))

n = 2035 bins (pre 835 / post 1200) · native step 7 d · 6% of bins interpolated (no NO3 sample).

plot_resid(D[["hbef_w1"]], STEP["hbef_w1"], paste(label["hbef_w1"], "— C–Q residual & derivative"))

Figure 1. hbef_w1 — C–Q model residual (green) + smoothed derivative (orange); phases shaded, dashed = disturbance. Subtitle gives the pre-fit C–Q exponent b. 

plot_wc(WC[["hbef_w1"]], STEP["hbef_w1"], "hbef_w1 — C–Q wavelet coherence")

Figure 2. hbef_w1 — C–Q wavelet coherence (time × period, period in days via the native step). Dashed guides = season / year / 2-year. 

plot_band_trace(TR[["hbef_w1"]], "hbef_w1 — C–Q synchrony at the annual band, through time")

Figure 3. hbef_w1 — annual-band C–Q metrics through time; COI edges blanked, phases shaded. 

emit_table(SM[["hbef_w1"]], "hbef_w1 — C–Q band synchrony, settled pre vs post")
hbef_w1 — C–Q band synchrony, settled pre vs post
phase n_blocks coherence xpower amp_ratio phi lag
pre 38 1 0.53 2.36 -0.02 -0.93
post 38 1 0.54 2.35 -0.04 -2.26 
plot_wave_bars(SM[["hbef_w1"]], "hbef_w1 — C–Q band metrics, pre vs post (±1 SE)")

Figure 4. hbef_w1 — pre vs post C–Q band metrics, ±1 SE.

hjandrews_GSWS10 — debris flow (pulse)

The coarsest grid (~3-weekly), so this panel is blank below ~3 months — an honest limit of its sampling. A physical channel disturbance; watch whether the annual-band C–Q amplitude/lag shift.

cat(prov_line(D[["hjandrews_GSWS10"]]))

n = 747 bins (pre 174 / post 573) · native step 21 d · 3% of bins interpolated (no NO3 sample).

plot_resid(D[["hjandrews_GSWS10"]], STEP["hjandrews_GSWS10"], paste(label["hjandrews_GSWS10"], "— C–Q residual & derivative"))

Figure 5. hjandrews_GSWS10 — C–Q residual (green) + derivative (orange); phases shaded, dashed = disturbance. 

plot_wc(WC[["hjandrews_GSWS10"]], STEP["hjandrews_GSWS10"], "hjandrews_GSWS10 — C–Q wavelet coherence")

Figure 6. hjandrews_GSWS10 — C–Q wavelet coherence. Blank below ~3 months (21-day sampling). 

plot_band_trace(TR[["hjandrews_GSWS10"]], "hjandrews_GSWS10 — C–Q synchrony at the annual band, through time")

Figure 7. hjandrews_GSWS10 — annual-band C–Q metrics through time. 

emit_table(SM[["hjandrews_GSWS10"]], "hjandrews_GSWS10 — C–Q band synchrony, settled pre vs post")

phase n_blocks coherence xpower amp_ratio phi lag
pre 43 0.98 0.36 0.23 -1.31 -75.88
post 43 0.98 0.36 0.25 -1.15 -66.76 
plot_wave_bars(SM[["hjandrews_GSWS10"]], "hjandrews_GSWS10 — C–Q band metrics, pre vs post (±1 SE)")

Figure 8. hjandrews_GSWS10 — pre vs post C–Q band metrics, ±1 SE.

fernow_WS-3 — chemical addition (chronic) · acidification

Chronic (NH₄)₂SO₄ acidification since 1989 — an ongoing manipulation acting directly on stream nitrogen, so “post” is an actively-forced regime. One flagged multi-month nitrate gap (~21% interpolated overall).

cat(prov_line(D[["fernow_WS-3"]]))

n = 1928 bins (pre 311 / post 1617) · native step 7 d · 21% of bins interpolated (no NO3 sample).

plot_resid(D[["fernow_WS-3"]], STEP["fernow_WS-3"], paste(label["fernow_WS-3"], "— C–Q residual & derivative"))

Figure 9. fernow_WS-3 — C–Q residual (green) + derivative (orange); phases shaded, dashed = disturbance. 

plot_wc(WC[["fernow_WS-3"]], STEP["fernow_WS-3"], "fernow_WS-3 — C–Q wavelet coherence")

fernow_WS-5 — timber harvest (pulse)

The clearcut is the classic nitrate-export disturbance — forest removal releases nitrate, so a C–Q change across 2007 is plausible here. One flagged multi-month gap (~9% interpolated).

cat(prov_line(D[["fernow_WS-5"]]))

n = 1473 bins (pre 835 / post 625) · native step 7 d · 9% of bins interpolated (no NO3 sample).

plot_resid(D[["fernow_WS-5"]], STEP["fernow_WS-5"], paste(label["fernow_WS-5"], "— C–Q residual & derivative"))

Figure 13. fernow_WS-5 — C–Q residual (green) + derivative (orange); phases shaded, dashed = disturbance.

Figure 14. fernow_WS-5 — C–Q wavelet coherence. 

plot_band_trace(TR[["fernow_WS-5"]], "fernow_WS-5 — C–Q synchrony at the annual band, through time")

plot_wave_bars(SM[["fernow_WS-5"]], "fernow_WS-5 — C–Q band metrics, pre vs post (±1 SE)")

Cross-site summary

Post-minus-pre change in the four C–Q band metrics per site, each Δ with its propagated SE. All sites share the physical annual band and identical metric scales, so they compare directly despite different native grids.

sm_all <- bind_rows(SM, .id = "site")
delta <- metrics_long(sm_all, c("site", "phase")) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = phase, values_from = c(value, se)) %>%
  mutate(delta = value_post - value_pre, delta_se = sqrt(se_pre^2 + se_post^2),
         site = factor(site, sites))
cat(kable(delta %>% transmute(site, metric = metric_labs[as.character(metric)],
                              pre = value_pre, post = value_post, delta, delta_se),
          digits = 2, caption = "pre → post change in C–Q band metrics, all sites (delta_se = propagated ±1 SE)"), sep = "\n")
pre → post change in C–Q band metrics, all sites (delta_se = propagated ±1 SE)
site metric pre post delta delta_se
hbef_w1 coherence 1.00 1.00 0.00 0.00
hbef_w1 cross power 0.53 0.54 0.00 0.04
hbef_w1 amp ratio 2.36 2.35 -0.02 0.19
hbef_w1 app. lag (d) -0.93 -2.26 -1.33 2.81
hjandrews_GSWS10 coherence 0.98 0.98 0.00 0.01
hjandrews_GSWS10 cross power 0.36 0.36 0.00 0.05
hjandrews_GSWS10 amp ratio 0.23 0.25 0.02 0.03
hjandrews_GSWS10 app. lag (d) -75.88 -66.76 9.12 19.04
fernow_WS-3 coherence 0.98 0.98 0.00 0.01
fernow_WS-3 cross power 0.14 0.14 0.00 0.02
fernow_WS-3 amp ratio 0.06 0.06 0.00 0.01
fernow_WS-3 app. lag (d) -17.83 -22.79 -4.96 17.84
fernow_WS-5 coherence 0.96 1.00 0.04 0.02
fernow_WS-5 cross power 0.23 0.21 -0.02 0.04
fernow_WS-5 amp ratio 0.08 0.09 0.01 0.02
fernow_WS-5 app. lag (d) -9.79 -3.94 5.84 13.74 
ggplot(delta, aes(site, delta, fill = site)) +
  geom_col() +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = delta - delta_se, ymax = delta + delta_se), width = 0.25, colour = "black", na.rm = TRUE) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, colour = "grey70") +
  facet_wrap(~ metric, scales = "free_y", labeller = as_labeller(metric_labs)) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2", guide = "none") +
  labs(title = "Post − pre change by C–Q band metric (free y axes; ±1 SE)", x = NULL, y = "post − pre") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 40, hjust = 1))

Figure 17. Post − pre change per site, faceted by band metric (free y), ±1 propagated SE. The C–Q read against P–Q (Analysis IV): a noisier, weaker coupling.

Takeaways — the wavelet on a less-causal coupling. Read against Analysis IV (P→Q): rainfall drives discharge almost mechanically, and there the normalized coherence saturated near 1 while cross-power/amplitude carried the story. C–Q is the harder case the study was built to reach — nitrate is only partly set by flow. The pre-fit exponent b (each residual subtitle) says whether a site is near-chemostatic (b≈0, nitrate roughly flow-independent) or chemodynamic; the band metrics say whether even that partial coupling is seasonally organized and whether the disturbance — especially the two that hit nitrogen directly (hbef Ca, fernow WS-3 acidification) — reshapes it. Where P→Q’s discharge channel was near-silent at the chemistry sites, C–Q is exactly where a signal can live. The method’s limits carry over intact: coherence remains the least trustworthy channel, the honest resolution is set by each site’s sampling (reported per panel), and a coarser record simply cannot see short periods — shown, not hidden. ```
